title: Mateo's Magic Lunch Box
synopsis: When Mateo discovers his new lunch box makes food appear and disappear, he embarks on a silly adventure at school that teaches him about sharing and friendship.

pages.text: Mateo loved his new lunch box. It was bright blue with silver stars all over it. His grandmother gave it to him for his first day at a new school. "This lunch box is very special," Grandma Rosa had said with a wink. "Take good care of it, mijo." Mateo didn't know what she meant. But he loved it anyway.

illustration.description: A happy Latino boy with dark hair and big brown eyes holding a magical-looking bright blue lunch box covered in shimmering silver stars. His grandmother, an elderly Latina woman with gray hair in a bun and kind eyes, stands beside him smiling warmly in a cozy kitchen with yellow walls.
illustration.alt: Mateo receiving his special lunch box from Grandma Rosa

pages.text: At breakfast, Mom packed his lunch. She put in a peanut butter sandwich, an apple, and some crackers. "Have a good day at school, sweetheart," Mom said. She gave him a big hug. Mateo's little sister Ana waved goodbye from her high chair. "Bye bye, Teo!" she giggled, throwing Cheerios in the air.

illustration.description: A warm kitchen scene with Mateo's mom, a Latina woman with long dark hair, packing lunch while Mateo watches. His toddler sister Ana, with curly dark hair, sits in a high chair throwing colorful Cheerios. The kitchen has bright yellow walls and cheerful morning sunlight streaming through windows.
illustration.alt: Mom packing Mateo's lunch while sister Ana plays with cereal

pages.text: On the school bus, Mateo sat next to a girl named Zara. She had curly red hair and freckles. "Cool lunch box!" she said. "Thanks," Mateo replied. "My grandma gave it to me." Just then, the lunch box made a tiny jingling sound. Mateo looked down. That was weird. Lunch boxes don't usually make noise.

illustration.description: Inside a yellow school bus, Mateo sits next to Zara, a friendly girl with bright red curly hair and freckles, both with big expressive eyes. The magical lunch box sits between them with tiny sparkles around it suggesting the mysterious jingling sound. Other diverse children are visible in nearby seats.
illustration.alt: Mateo and Zara on the school bus with the jingling lunch box

pages.text: At lunchtime, Mateo opened his lunch box. His eyes went wide. Inside was not just his sandwich and apple. There were also cookies, a juice box, and a banana! "Where did all this food come from?" he whispered. His lunch box jingled again, very quietly. The stars on it seemed to twinkle.

illustration.description: Mateo sits at a colorful school cafeteria table with his mouth open in surprise, staring into his magical lunch box overflowing with extra food. The blue lunch box glows softly with twinkling silver stars. Sunlight streams through cafeteria windows creating a magical atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Mateo discovering extra food in his magical lunch box

pages.text: Zara sat down next to him. "Wow! Your mom packed a lot of food," she said. But Mateo knew his mom had only packed three things. He looked around the cafeteria. Some kids didn't have much lunch. Marcus, a boy with glasses, only had a small bag of pretzels. Lin, a girl with long black hair, had forgotten her lunch completely.

illustration.description: A diverse school cafeteria scene with Mateo and Zara at a table surrounded by other children. Marcus, a Black boy with glasses, holds a small bag of pretzels looking sad. Lin, an Asian girl with long black hair, sits with an empty space in front of her looking worried. The cafeteria has bright colors and large windows.
illustration.alt: Children in the cafeteria with varying amounts of lunch

pages.text: Mateo had an idea. Maybe his special lunch box wanted to help! He walked over to Marcus. "Want some cookies?" he asked. "Really?" Marcus smiled big. "Thank you!" Next, Mateo gave Lin his extra banana. "You're so nice, Mateo," Lin said. The lunch box jingled happily.

illustration.description: Mateo stands between Marcus and Lin, sharing food from his magical lunch box. Marcus beams with joy holding cookies, while Lin smiles gratefully accepting the banana. The lunch box sits nearby with gentle sparkles around it, and other diverse children watch the kind gesture with happy expressions.
illustration.alt: Mateo sharing his extra food with Marcus and Lin

pages.text: After lunch, something strange happened. Mateo put his empty lunch box in his backpack. But when he got to art class, he heard jingling again. He peeked inside his backpack. The lunch box was full of art supplies! There were crayons, markers, and stickers. "This lunch box is definitely magic," Mateo whispered.

illustration.description: In a bright art classroom with colorful posters on the walls, Mateo peers into his backpack with amazement. His magical lunch box now contains rainbow-colored art supplies that seem to glow softly. Other children sit at art tables in the background, and the teacher, a kind-looking woman with short brown hair, arranges supplies.
illustration.alt: Mateo discovering art supplies in his magic lunch box

pages.text: But then the magic got a little crazy. During math class, Mateo opened the lunch box to find toy dinosaurs inside! "Roar!" said a tiny T-Rex as it climbed out. Mrs. Chen, his teacher, turned around. "Did someone say something?" she asked. Mateo quickly closed the lunch box. The dinosaur was gone!

illustration.description: In a classroom with math posters and a whiteboard, Mateo sits at his desk with wide, panicked eyes as a tiny, colorful toy T-Rex emerges from his lunch box. Mrs. Chen, an Asian woman with short black hair and glasses, turns from the whiteboard with a curious expression. Other diverse children work at their desks unaware of the magic.
illustration.alt: A tiny dinosaur emerging from Mateo's lunch box during math class

pages.text: In the library, the lunch box filled with books that started reading themselves! "Once upon a time," whispered a fairy tale book. The librarian, Mr. Johnson, looked around confused. "I thought I heard something," he said, scratching his head. Mateo was getting worried. This magic was getting out of control!

illustration.description: In a cozy school library with tall bookshelves, Mateo sits at a small table while magical books float around his lunch box, their pages fluttering open. Mr. Johnson, a friendly Black man with a beard and cardigan, looks puzzled in the background. Soft golden light filters through library windows creating a whimsical, magical atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Magical books floating around Mateo's lunch box in the library

pages.text: By recess, Mateo was nervous. What if the lunch box did something really crazy? He decided to leave it in his backpack. But during kickball, he heard jingling from the sidelines. His backpack was bouncing! "Oh no," Mateo said. He ran to check on it.

illustration.description: On a sunny playground with green grass and playground equipment, children play kickball while Mateo's backpack bounces mysteriously by the fence. Mateo runs toward it with a worried expression, his hair flowing in the wind. Other diverse children continue playing, unaware of the magical chaos.
illustration.alt: Mateo's bouncing backpack during recess kickball game

pages.text: Inside the lunch box were... puppies! Three tiny golden retriever puppies were yapping and trying to climb out. "Woof! Woof!" they barked. Kids started to notice. "Are those real puppies?" asked Zara. "Where did they come from?" asked Marcus. Mateo didn't know what to say!

illustration.description: Three adorable tiny golden retriever puppies with big eyes and wagging tails climb out of the magical blue lunch box while Mateo looks panicked. Zara and Marcus peer over his shoulders with amazement, and other diverse children gather around in a circle, all with expressions of wonder and excitement.
illustration.alt: Tiny puppies emerging from the magic lunch box surrounded by amazed children

pages.text: Soon, all the kids gathered around. Everyone wanted to pet the puppies. But then the puppies started to disappear, one by one! "Poof!" went the first puppy. "Poof!" went the second. "Where are they going?" cried Lin. The last puppy licked Mateo's hand and vanished too. The kids looked very confused.

illustration.description: A crowd of diverse children with various expressions of confusion and wonder watch as the last golden puppy fades away in sparkles and glitter while licking Mateo's outstretched hand. The magical lunch box sits open nearby, still twinkling with silver stars against the playground background.
illustration.alt: The last puppy disappearing in sparkles as confused children watch

pages.text: That afternoon, Mateo called Grandma Rosa. "Grandma, my lunch box is doing crazy things!" he said. Grandma laughed. "The lunch box has a good heart, mijo. It wants to help and make people happy. But you have to guide it with your own good heart." Mateo thought about this.

illustration.description: Mateo sits on his bed in his colorful bedroom, talking on a phone with a worried but hopeful expression. On the phone screen, Grandma Rosa appears with twinkling eyes and a knowing smile. The magical lunch box sits on his desk nearby, glowing softly with gentle silver starlight.
illustration.alt: Mateo talking to Grandma Rosa on the phone about the magic lunch box

pages.text: The next day at lunch, Mateo tried something different. He held the lunch box gently and thought about helping his friends. "Please give me just enough extra food to share," he whispered. The lunch box jingled softly. When he opened it, there was exactly one extra sandwich and one extra apple.

illustration.description: In the school cafeteria, Mateo sits peacefully with his hands gently placed on the magical lunch box, eyes closed in concentration. Soft, warm golden light surrounds him and the lunch box glows with gentle silver stars. Other children eat lunch nearby in the cheerful, sunlit cafeteria.
illustration.alt: Mateo concentrating while holding his magic lunch box

pages.text: Mateo shared the extra food with two new kids who had just started school. They were twins named Sam and Sofia. "Thank you so much," said Sam. "You're really kind," added Sofia. The lunch box gave a happy little jingle. Mateo smiled. Now he understood how the magic worked. It was all about sharing and caring for others.

illustration.description: Mateo sits at a lunch table with the twins Sam and Sofia, who have different ethnic backgrounds and grateful expressions. He's sharing food with them while his magic lunch box sits contentedly nearby with a gentle glow. The scene shows friendship and kindness in the bright, welcoming school cafeteria.
illustration.alt: Mateo sharing lunch with new students Sam and Sofia

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: Who gave Mateo the special lunch box?
questions.explanation: Grandma Rosa gave Mateo the magical lunch box for his first day at a new school.

questions.question: What was the first magical thing the lunch box did?
questions.explanation: The first magic happened at lunch when Mateo found extra cookies, juice, and a banana that his mom didn't pack.

questions.question: What did Mateo share with Marcus?
questions.explanation: Mateo gave Marcus some cookies when he noticed Marcus only had a small bag of pretzels.

questions.question: What happened to the puppies?
questions.explanation: The three puppies disappeared one by one, going 'Poof!' and vanishing in front of all the confused children.

questions.question: How did Mateo learn to control the magic?
choices: By asking nicely and thinking about helping
By closing the lunch box tightly
questions.explanation: Grandma Rosa told him the lunch box responds to a good heart, so Mateo learned to ask gently and think about helping others.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.text: Mateo loved his new ____ box. It was bright ____ with silver stars. His ____ gave it to him. At lunch, he found ____ food inside. He decided to ____ with his friends.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: The magic lunch box made ____ appear during recess. The children were very ____. But the animals started to ____ one by one! Mateo called his ____ for help. She told him to use his good ____.
